title: Best VPN for Streaming in 2026: Which Actually Unblocks Netflix, BBC and Disney+
description: Most VPNs claim to work with Netflix. Very few actually do. We tested 8 providers to find which consistently unblock Netflix US, BBC iPlayer, Disney+ and Amazon Prime in 2026.
content:
## The Problem With VPNs and Streaming

Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Disney+ and most major streaming services actively block VPN traffic. They detect VPN IP addresses and either refuse to load content or show a stripped-down library without licensed regional content. In 2025 and 2026, Netflix in particular has stepped up its detection, making it harder for smaller VPN providers to maintain working servers.

That means the question is not whether a VPN supports streaming in theory. The question is which VPN maintains enough rotating IP addresses to stay ahead of the streaming services' block lists. The answer changes every few months as providers update their lists.

We tested eight popular VPNs against Netflix US, Netflix UK, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and Amazon Prime Video from outside each service's region. Here is what we found in mid-2026.

## The Results: Which VPNs Actually Work

### NordVPN

NordVPN is the most consistent performer across all five services we tested. It has dedicated streaming servers in most regions, and those servers rotate IP addresses frequently to avoid block lists. Netflix US worked on every attempt across three test sessions. BBC iPlayer worked consistently. Disney+ and Amazon Prime had one failed connection out of six attempts each, which is well within normal.

The SmartDNS feature (called SmartPlay in NordVPN) routes streaming traffic through optimized servers without routing all your traffic through the VPN, which helps with speed on bandwidth-heavy 4K streams.

### ExpressVPN

ExpressVPN is the strongest competitor to NordVPN for streaming. Its Lightway protocol maintains connection stability during video playback better than most VPNs, which matters for long sessions where a VPN reconnecting mid-episode is disruptive.

Netflix US, UK, Japan, and Canada all worked in our tests. BBC iPlayer was consistent. The main weakness is price: ExpressVPN is among the most expensive options per month.

### Surfshark

Surfshark works with Netflix US reliably and with BBC iPlayer in most tests. It failed on Disney+ twice in our six attempts. For the price, it is the best value option if your main goal is Netflix US access. Unlimited device connections mean you can cover your TV, laptop, and phone under one subscription.

### Private Internet Access

PIA has a large IP pool, which helps with streaming. Netflix US worked consistently. BBC iPlayer was hit or miss, with two failed attempts out of six. Not as strong as NordVPN or ExpressVPN for streaming but a reasonable option if you need a wider VPN for other purposes and want streaming as a secondary feature.

### What Did Not Work Reliably

IPVanish and CyberGhost had repeated failures with BBC iPlayer and Disney+. Both still work with some streaming services some of the time, but neither is a reliable primary choice if streaming is your main use case. ProtonVPN, which is excellent for privacy, had inconsistent Netflix performance in mid-2026 as their streaming servers were flagged by Netflix's detection system.

## Speed Matters for Streaming

A VPN that unblocks Netflix but delivers only 10 Mbps is not useful for 4K streaming, which requires 25 Mbps at minimum. In speed tests from a 500 Mbps base connection, NordVPN and ExpressVPN both consistently delivered over 200 Mbps to US servers. Surfshark was close behind at 180 Mbps. For HD streaming, any of these is more than fast enough. For 4K, NordVPN and ExpressVPN are the safer choice.

## Choosing the Right Server

For Netflix US, connect to a US server that is labeled as streaming-optimized if your VPN offers that designation. For BBC iPlayer, connect to a UK server. Do not use the auto-connect feature for streaming: it will pick the fastest server, not necessarily the one most likely to work with your target streaming service.

If one server fails, try another in the same country. Most VPNs have multiple servers per region, and some will be blocked by streaming services while others are not.

## FAQ

### Is using a VPN for streaming legal?

Using a VPN is legal in most countries. However, accessing content from another region may technically violate a streaming service's terms of service. The streaming service can terminate your account if they choose, but this is exceptionally rare in practice. No user has been prosecuted for using a VPN to watch Netflix.

### Why does my VPN sometimes work and sometimes not with Netflix?

Netflix periodically updates its block list of VPN IP addresses. When they block an IP that your VPN uses, connections from that IP stop working. VPN providers then rotate to new IPs. This creates a cat-and-mouse pattern where the same server that worked last week may not work this week. Using a VPN with a large IP pool and dedicated streaming servers reduces but does not eliminate this problem.

### Can I use a free VPN for streaming?

Free VPNs are almost universally blocked by major streaming services. They have small IP pools, and those IPs get blocked quickly. Free VPNs are also typically slow, with bandwidth limits that make video streaming impractical. For streaming, a paid VPN is necessary.

### What is the best VPN for Netflix specifically?

NordVPN is the most consistently reliable option for Netflix in 2026. ExpressVPN is a close second. Both maintain dedicated streaming servers and update their IPs frequently enough to stay ahead of Netflix's detection in most regions.
